anti_triggers: Strong consistency required for all reads
preconditions: Message Bus or Event Store availability
Compensation logic implemented in each service

triggers: Temporal queries (view state at any time)

preconditions: Isolation of thread pools or process resources
Key hashing or random salt logic

triggers: Cost awareness with high availability need

zta_strict.name: Strict Zero Trust (Maximum Security)
zta_strict.risk: High operational friction / Availability false positives

triggers: High risk of internal movement (lateral shift)
anti_triggers: Early-stage startups with no compliance burden

triggers: Need for low friction user experience

secret_vault_centralized.name: Centralized Secret Vault & Rotation
secret_vault_centralized.risk: Single Point of Failure / High OpEx

secret_leasing_expiration.name: Leasing & Dynamic Expiration
secret_leasing_expiration.risk: Dependency on SDK/API / Potential availability impact on expiration

triggers: Requirement to minimize session hijacking risk
preconditions: Vault API support for dynamic secrets

secret_vault_standard.name: Standard Cloud-Native Vault
secret_vault_standard.risk: Medium exposure if rotation is not configured

triggers: Primary usage within one cloud provider

crypto_high_assurance.name: High-Assurance Applied Cryptography
crypto_high_assurance.risk: Performance overhead / High complexity / Key management errors

success_metrics.data_protection_coverage: At rest, In transit, In use

crypto_standard_enterprise.name: Standard Enterprise Cryptography
crypto_standard_enterprise.risk: Partial encryption coverage / Slow key rotation

tm_stride.name: STRIDE Threat Modeling
tm_stride.risk: Superficial analysis / High false negative rate if not validated

triggers: New feature with sensitive data handling
anti_triggers: Internal research tools with no real data

success_metrics.coverage: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Info Disclosure, DoS, Elevation of Privilege

tm_risk_quantification.name: Risk Quantification & ROI
tm_risk_quantification.risk: Data inaccuracy in financial impact estimates

triggers: High-value PII or financial data processing

cache_static_global.name: Global Static Caching (CDN/Edge)
cache_static_global.risk: Stale content / Cache invalidation propagation delay

triggers: Need for minimal TTFB across multiple regions
anti_triggers: Dynamic content with zero-tolerance for staleness

triggers: High-frequency data access (>1k req/s per service)

cache_db_materialized.name: Database-level Caching (Materialized Views)
cache_db_materialized.risk: Write amplification during refresh / Stale aggregations

triggers: Complex joins and aggregations on large tables
Slow query hotspots on primary DB
anti_triggers: Frequently changing writes on the underlying tables

msg_bus_kafka.name: High-throughput Event Bus (Kafka)
msg_bus_kafka.risk: Zookeeper/Kraft complexity / Replication lag / SPOF if not 3-node min

triggers: Need for strict ordering via partitioning
anti_triggers: Small dev teams with no Kafka expertise

msg_broker_rabbitmq.name: Intelligent Message Broker (RabbitMQ)
msg_broker_rabbitmq.risk: Memory pressure on high concurrency / Erlang-based debugging complexity

msg_queue_sqs.name: Serverless Managed Queue (AWS SQS / GCP PubSub)
msg_queue_sqs.risk: Limited ordering (unless FIFO) / Scaling latency in extreme surges

ec_vector_clocks.name: Vector Clocks Consistency
ec_vector_clocks.risk: Conflict resolution complexity / Logical clock synchronization

triggers: Need to detect concurrent updates in distributed systems

ec_crdts.name: Conflict-free Replicated Data Types (CRDTs)
ec_crdts.risk: High memory consumption / Complexity in designing custom types

anti_triggers: Large BLOB updates where state-based CRDTs are inefficient
preconditions: Operations must be commutative, associative, and idempotent

ec_lww.name: Last-Write-Wins (LWW) Consistency
ec_lww.risk: Silent data loss on concurrent updates / NTP clock skew impact

triggers: Systems where losing concurrent updates is tolerable

ma_single_tagging.name: Single Account with Tagging Governance
ma_single_tagging.risk: Blast radius in case of compromise / Accidental resource deletion / No hard cost isolation

anti_triggers: Production workloads with high blast radius risk

ma_product_isolation.name: Multi-account Isolation by Product
ma_product_isolation.risk: Identity management complexity / Inter-account connectivity (VPC Peering/Transit Gateway)

triggers: Need for hard isolation of security and cost

ma_full_env_isolation.name: Full Isolation by Product & Environment
ma_full_env_isolation.risk: Replication of base infra overhead / Deployment pipeline complexity

triggers: Need for 100% isolation of Dev/Test from Prod

ma_landing_zone_scp.name: Landing Zone with SCP Governance
ma_landing_zone_scp.risk: Incorrect SCP blocking critical business operations / Steep learning curve

anti_triggers: Projects with no centralized IT/Cloud team
preconditions: Experience with SCPs and guardrail concepts
Platform Team or Center of Excellence (CoE) in place

sm_mtls_only.name: Minimal Service Mesh (mTLS focus)
sm_mtls_only.risk: Certificate management overhead / Sidecar lifecycle issues

requires: Service Mesh Control Plane (Linkerd/Istio in minimal profile)

triggers: Security mandate for encryption in transit
anti_triggers: Environments where network-level encryption is sufficient

sm_full_istio.name: Enterprise Service Mesh (Full Istio)
sm_full_istio.risk: Configuration complexity / Latency introduction / Upgrade difficulty

triggers: Deep observability and distributed tracing requirement

sm_linkerd_light.name: Performance-Optimized Service Mesh (Linkerd)
sm_linkerd_light.risk: Fewer advanced features compared to Istio

triggers: Need for golden metrics and mTLS with minimal configuration
anti_triggers: Complex Lua scripts or WASM extension needs (use Istio)
preconditions: Linkerd CLI and cluster check passed

plat_serverless_lambda.name: Serverless Functions (FaaS)
plat_serverless_lambda.risk: Cold starts / Vendor lock-in / Execution time limits / Debugging complexity

requires: Cloud Provider Function Service (AWS Lambda/Azure Functions)

triggers: Highly variable workloads / Unpredictable traffic peaks
Cost optimization for low-volume or intermittent tasks
anti_triggers: Services requiring ultra-low latency consistency (<50ms consistently)
preconditions: Fast startup runtime (Go, Rust, Node.js optimized)

plat_containers_k8s.name: Containerized Microservices (Docker/K8s)
plat_containers_k8s.risk: Orchestration complexity / Scaling lag / Management of underlying nodes

triggers: Need for full control over runtime and environment
Integration with advanced Service Mesh features
anti_triggers: Extremely low traffic services where idle costs are prohibitive

fo_reserved_savings.name: Reserved Instances & Savings Plans
fo_reserved_savings.risk: Underutilization if workload changes / Long-term contract commitment

triggers: Stable workload with usage history > 6 months
Predictable base load for critical services
Requirement for significant cost reduction (up to 72%)
anti_triggers: Highly dynamic environments with short-lived projects
Organizations with severe cash flow constraints

fo_spot_instances.name: Spot Instances Strategy
fo_spot_instances.risk: Service interruption with short notice (2 mins) / Requires robust fallback logic

triggers: Scalable container clusters with mixed instance types
anti_triggers: User-facing critical APIs without redundant capacity
Jobs that cannot be resumed / No checkpointing
Databases or stateful services without replication
preconditions: Fallback to on-demand/RI if spot is unavailable

fo_dynamic_rightsizing.name: Dynamic Rightsizing & Auto-scaling
fo_dynamic_rightsizing.risk: Performance degradation if sized too small / Throttling during sudden spikes

triggers: Detected low utilization (<30% CPU/Mem) on over-provisioned resources
anti_triggers: Workloads with extremely unpredictable, sudden mega-spikes (use Serverless)
Legacy apps with slow startup times

fo_showback_chargeback.name: Showback & Chargeback Governance
fo_showback_chargeback.risk: Inter-departmental friction / Difficulty in attributing shared resources (e.g., Transit Gateway)

triggers: Requirement for unit economics (cost per transaction/user)
Need for cultural change towards cost awareness
anti_triggers: Small startups with a single product and team
Monolithic finance structure with no team-level budgets

zta_identity_proxy.name: Identity-aware Proxy (IAP) & MFA
zta_identity_proxy.risk: Friction if MFA is too aggressive / Single point of failure for access

triggers: Need to replace traditional VPNs with context-aware access

zta_micro_segmentation.name: Service-to-Service Micro-segmentation
zta_micro_segmentation.risk: High complexity / Potential breakage if policies are too tight

triggers: Need to limit the impact of a compromised service
anti_triggers: Small clusters with low security risk

zta_continuous_auth.name: Continuous Authentication & Device Posture
zta_continuous_auth.risk: Poor UX if risk scoring is too sensitive / Device agent maintenance

triggers: High risk of session hijacking in sensitive environments
Need for real-time risk evaluation of every request
anti_triggers: Managed corporate devices only (can use standard auth)

secret_multi_region_sync.name: Multi-region Secret Sync & DR
secret_multi_region_sync.risk: Split-brain in secret synchronization / Replication latency

triggers: Critical RTO/RPO for identity and access
anti_triggers: High tolerance for secret availability downtime
preconditions: Support for cross-region replication in Vault

requires: Managed KMS (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault)

triggers: Financial data requiring separation of duties
Need for automatic key rotation every 30-90 days

crypto_in_transit_mtls.name: Mutual TLS (mTLS) for Microservices
crypto_in_transit_mtls.risk: Certificate expiration causing service outages / Complexity in debugging

requires: Service Mesh (Istio, Linkerd) or Centralized CA

triggers: Need to authenticate both client and server automatically
anti_triggers: Simple internal tools with no sensitive traffic
preconditions: Cluster-wide CA or Service Mesh control plane

crypto_in_use_confidential.name: Confidential Computing (TEE/Enclaves)
crypto_in_use_confidential.risk: High code complexity / Vendor lock-in / Major performance hit

requires: HW support (Intel SGX, AWS Nitro Enclaves)

triggers: Processing ultra-sensitive financial/health data in use
ML model training on private data

crypto_hsm_dedicated.name: Dedicated HSM for Root Keys
crypto_hsm_dedicated.operating_cost: High (Fixed monthly cost per HSM unit)
crypto_hsm_dedicated.risk: High management overhead / Specialized expertise required

triggers: Banking / Payment gateway root key requirements
anti_triggers: Shared KMS is sufficient for risk profile

tm_stride_quantified.name: STRIDE Quantified (S-T-R-I-D-E)
tm_stride_quantified.risk: Human error in impact estimations / Subjective probability

triggers: Need to identify specific threat surfaces per component
anti_triggers: Internal tools with no external exposure

tm_attack_tree_economic.name: Economic Attack Trees
tm_attack_tree_economic.risk: Complexity explosion / Outdated trees as infra evolves

triggers: Need to identify the 'cheapest' path for an attacker
anti_triggers: Monolithic applications with single entry point

tm_risk_quantification_ale.name: Financial Risk Quantification (ALE/SLE)
tm_risk_quantification_ale.risk: Inaccurate financial data / Overestimation of probabilities

requires: Financial impact data (GDPR fines, reputation cost)

triggers: Budget priority setting for security investments
anti_triggers: Projects with negligible financial breach impact
Very low budget constraints where baseline is only option
preconditions: Clarity on ARO (Annual Rate of Occurrence)

dora_ict_risk_mgmt.name: DORA ICT Risk Management Framework
dora_ict_risk_mgmt.risk: Regulatory non-compliance leading to fines / Operational silos

triggers: Need for formalized risk management framework (Art. 6)
anti_triggers: Non-EU operations with no local footprint

dora_art8_prevention.name: DORA Protection & Prevention (Art. 8)
dora_art8_prevention.risk: Insufficient technical depth / Legacy system incompatibility

triggers: Need for robust access control and encryption
Requirement for continuous monitoring of vulnerabilities
anti_triggers: Internal lab environments with no production data

triggers: High availability mandates for critical functions
Need for verified backup restore capabilities

dora_art11_major_reporting.name: DORA Incident Reporting (Art. 11-12)
dora_art11_major_reporting.risk: Reporting delays leading to severe regulatory sanctions

triggers: Mandatory timeline for notification to authorities
Requirement for root cause analysis report
anti_triggers: Minor internal bugs with no client impact

tprm_concentration_risk.name: Concentration Risk Assessment
tprm_concentration_risk.risk: Systemic failure due to single-vendor dependency (Lock-in)

triggers: Critical SaaS with no viable manual fallback

tprm_exit_strategy.name: Exit Strategy Viability (Art. 28)
tprm_exit_strategy.risk: Contractual or technical lock-in preventing provider switch

triggers: Mandatory DORA requirement for critical ICT services
Provider termination warning or quality degradation
anti_triggers: Internal bespoke systems with no external dependencies

resilience_testing_tlpt.name: Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT)
resilience_testing_tlpt.operating_cost: Very High (External intelligence + Red/Blue team exercises)
resilience_testing_tlpt.risk: Operational disruption during testing / Intelligence leaks

resilience_testing_red_team.name: Red Team Adversarial Simulation
resilience_testing_red_team.risk: Unexpected system behavior / Security control bypass exposure

triggers: Need to test detection and containment speed (TTD/TTC)

preconditions: Defined 'critical success factors' for the scenario

unified_risk_engine.name: Unified Risk & Resilience Scoring Engine (URRSE)
unified_risk_engine.risk: Incorrect scoring leading to misallocated investment

anti_triggers: Small-scale internal tools with no regulatory impact
preconditions: Validated input from other DORA pillars

financial_exposure_model.name: Consolidated Financial Exposure (ALE_Total)
financial_exposure_model.risk: Underestimated financial impact of multi-pillar failures

triggers: Need for absolute monetary risk value

saas_resilience_engine.name: Risk & Resilience SaaS Architecture
saas_resilience_engine.operating_cost: High (Cloud infra + Specialized DBs + Security compliance)
saas_resilience_engine.risk: Data leakage in multi-tenant environments / Integration failures

triggers: Scalable risk management platform for financial sector

multi_tenant_isolation_strict.name: Strict Multi-tenant Isolation
multi_tenant_isolation_strict.risk: Increased operational complexity / Higher cost per account

triggers: Handling sensitive banking/financial data in SaaS
Need to model complex attack trees (Graph)
Requirement for high-performance risk trend metrics (Time-series)

dora_art7_identification.name: ICT Assets & Functions Identification (Art. 7)
dora_art7_identification.risk: Undetected shadow IT / Unknown critical dependencies

preconditions: Defined criteria for 'Critical or Important' functions

dora_art9_detection.name: ICT Detection & Anomalies Monitoring (Art. 9)
dora_art9_detection.risk: Delayed incident detection / Control silent failure

dora_art10_recovery_advanced.name: ICT Response & Recovery (Art. 10)
dora_art10_recovery_advanced.risk: Service interruption beyond RTO / Financial instability

dora_art11_reporting_auto.name: ICT Incident Reporting (Art. 11-12)
dora_art11_reporting_auto.risk: Regulatory non-compliance due to late notification

major_ict_incident.name: Major ICT-related Incident (DORA)
major_ict_incident.risk: Fines / License revocation / Severe client impact

triggers: High number of affected clients (> X%)

milestones.final_report: < 72 hours or upon closure (root cause + final impact)

resilience_testing_advanced.name: Advanced Resilience Testing (DORA Art. 24-27)
resilience_testing_advanced.risk: Operational instability during tests / Side effects in production

tiber_eu_framework.name: TIBER-EU Framework (Threat Intelligence-led Ethical Red Teaming)
tiber_eu_framework.operating_cost: Very High (Threat Intel + Red Team + Blue Team coordination)
tiber_eu_framework.risk: Sensitive data exposure / Uncontrolled service failure

threat_simulation_scenario.name: Threat Simulation Scenario
common_scenarios: BGP Route Hijacking / Network Latency Spike

tprm_critical_provider.name: Critical ICT Third-Party Provider (DORA Art. 28-30)
tprm_critical_provider.risk: Concentration risk / Undetected supply chain failure

chain_dependency_risk.name: Chained Dependency Risk (Digital Supply Chain)
chain_dependency_risk.risk: Invisible systemic failure across service clusters

triggers: Incident in shared infrastructure (e.g. major cloud region)
Security breach in common library or sub-provider

dependency_graph_model.name: Dependency Graph Model
dependency_graph_model.risk: Stale data leading to incorrect risk prioritization

impact_score_metric.name: Dependency Impact Score
impact_score_metric.definition: A quantitative value (0-1) representing the probability and severity of business disruption if the dependent node fails.

pseudonymization_strategy.name: Pseudonymization Strategy (GDPR Art. 4.5)
pseudonymization_strategy.risk: Reversibility via data cross-referencing or key breach

crypto_shredding.name: Crypto-Shredding (KMS Key Destruction)
crypto_shredding.risk: Key loss = total data loss / Key leakage = restoration possible
mechanisms: Per-user or per-tenant encryption keys
Destruction of key material in KMS (Art. 17 compatibility)
Hardware Security Module (HSM) enforcement

automated_ttl_enforcement.name: Automated TTL Lifecycle
automated_ttl_enforcement.risk: Misconfiguration leading to premature data loss

relationships.integration: S3 Lifecycle / DynamoDB TTL / Database Garbage Collection

immutable_deletion_audit.name: Immutable Deletion Audit Trail
mechanisms: Write-Once-Read-Many (WORM) storage for logs
Digital signatures on deletion evidence
KMS-backed audit sealing

dpia_automated_scoring.name: Automated DPIA Scoring Engine
dpia_automated_scoring.risk: False sense of security if triggers are poorly mapped

algorithmic_impact_assessment.name: Algorithmic Impact Assessment
mechanisms: Bias detection in training sets
Explainability (XAI) requirements for legal effects
Human-in-the-loop validation

triggers: Automated scoring for credit or insurance

risk_mitigation_mapping.name: Automated Risk Mitigation Mapping
mechanisms: Lookup of high-risk findings to architectural controls
Automatic injection of mitigation components
Residual risk quantification logic

tia_automation.name: Automated Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA)
tia_automation.risk: Inaccurate jurisdictional assessment if laws change
mechanisms: Country adequacy database lookup
Jurisdictional risk scoring
Assessment of recipient's local surveillance laws

success_metrics.legal_readiness: TIA report available for all non-EEA flows

eu_controlled_kms.name: EU-Controlled Key Management (Sovereign Cloud)
eu_controlled_kms.risk: Latency increase due to cross-region key calls
mechanisms: Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) with EU-based HSMs
External Key Store (XKS) integration
Strict policy preventing decryption by US-based providers

cross_border_tokenization.name: Privacy-Preserving Cross-Border Tokenization
mechanisms: De-identification before EEE exit
Vault-based mapping stored exclusively in EU
Transparent proxy for encrypted data flows

immutable_audit_logging.name: Immutable Regulatory Audit Logging
mechanisms: WORM (Write-Once-Read-Many) storage for logs
Cryptographic hashing of log chains
Tamper-proof alerting for log deletion attempts

dpo_evidence_vault.name: DPO Automated Evidence Vault
mechanisms: Automated treatment data mapping
Real-time retention adherence tracking
Centralized DPIA and TIA document repository

isolated_financial_segment.name: Isolated Financial Infrastructure Segment
mechanisms: Network isolation for accounting and payment systems
Strict ingress/egress filtering for financial traffic
Dedicated audit logging per segment

dual_control_op.name: Dual Control Operations (Two-Man Rule)
mechanisms: Mandatory second-person approval for critical configuration changes
Segregation of duties (SoD) enforcement
Verifiable approval trails for every production change

change_management_governance.name: Automated Technical Change Management
mechanisms: GitOps-based production deployment workflows
Automated rollback plans for every change
Traceable Board-level evidence for system reliability

sca_multi_factor.name: Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) Multi-Factor
mechanisms: Possession-based (mobile app, hardware token)
Knowledge-based (PIN, password)
Inherence-based (biometrics)
Mandatory 2-of-3 factor challenge for regulated payments

dynamic_linking_vault.name: PSD2 Dynamic Linking Cryptographic Vault
mechanisms: Cryptographic binding of amount and beneficiary to authentication code
Tamper-proof display of transaction details during approval
Non-repudiation of authorized financial transactions

3ds2_protocol.name: EMV 3-D Secure 2.0 (3DS2) Protocol
mechanisms: Risk-based authentication flow for ecommerce
Native mobile authentication support
frictionless checkout for low-risk transactions

tra_risk_engine.name: Transaction Risk Analysis (TRA) Engine
mechanisms: Real-time fraud rate monitoring against regulatory thresholds
Automated SCA exemption justification (Art. 18 RTS)
Risk scoring based on device, behavior, and history

adaptive_authentication.name: Adaptive & Behavioral Authentication
mechanisms: Behavioral biometrics (typing speed, touch patterns)
Device fingerprinting and velocity checks
Elastic challenge levels based on real-time risk scoring

kyc_automated_biometrics.name: Automated KYC & Biometric Identity Verification
mechanisms: Scanning and OCR for government-issued IDs
Liveness detection for remote identity proofing
Biometric matching against extracted facial images
SEPBLAC-compliant remote onboarding protocols

ubo_registry_immutable.name: Immutable Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO) Registry
mechanisms: Trazability of corporate structures > 25% participation
Immutable storage for UBO identity documents
10-year document retention for PBC/FT compliance

transaction_monitoring_realtime.name: Real-Time AML Transaction Monitoring
mechanisms: Automated detection of smurfing, layering, and structuring
Real-time scoring against sanctioned country lists
Risk-based anomaly detection for unusual volume spikes

aml_rba_engine.name: AML Risk-Based Approach (RBA) Engine
mechanisms: Quantitative scoring of geography, activity, and channel risk
Automated categorization into Low, Medium, High risk tiers
Deterministic triggering of Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD)

sepblac_reporting_connector.name: Automated SEPBLAC Reporting Connector
mechanisms: Secure channel for Suspicious Activity Report (SAR/ROS) filing
Automated systematically reported operation logs
Immutable evidence vault for regulatory inspections

investor_suitability_hub.name: MiFID II Investor Suitability Hub
mechanisms: Automated suitability/knowledge testing engine
Real-time investor risk profiling (MiFID II compliant)
Deterministic portfolio rebalancing based on risk scores
Algorithmic explainability trails for investment advice

crowdfunding_escrow_segregation.name: ECSP Crowdfunding Asset Segregation
mechanisms: Automated segregation of client funds from platform assets
Cooling-off period enforcement (4 days) for non-experienced investors
KIIS document validation and versioning control

crypto_cold_storage_hsm.name: Hardened Crypto Custody (Cold Storage + HSM)
mechanisms: Hardware Security Module (HSM) based private key management
Offline cold storage policy for > 90% of digital assets
Multi-signature (Multi-sig) approval workflows for withdrawals
Physical/logical segregation of wallet infrastructure

mica_governance_framework.name: MiCA Crypto Governance Framework
mechanisms: Market in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) whitepaper versioning
CASP (Crypto-Asset Service Provider) procedural alignment
Prudential requirement monitoring and alerting

market_abuse_monitoring.name: Real-Time Market Abuse Monitoring
mechanisms: Detection of wash trading, spoofing, and layering
Algorithmic order flow analysis for suspicious patterns
Automated alert escalation to CNMV/Regulator connectors
